checks.a_analysis: The output consists of a title and three image elements. The title 'Building Footprint Update Status' directly matches the text at the top of the source PDF. The image alt texts are descriptive of the logos and map shown in the source and do not contain any fabricated information or invented body text.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, addresses, or URLs) present in the source PDF or the converted output. Therefore, no fabrication occurred.

The source document is a single-page slide. The converted output includes the main heading and the three primary visual elements (two logos and the map graphic) as images. No pages or major sections were omitted.
The source document does not contain any formal tables. The legend on the map is part of the map image and is described in the map's alt text in the output.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output captures the main title as text and provides comprehensive alt text for the images. The alt text for the second image correctly summarizes the information contained in the map's title and legend ('Risk Building Footprint Refresh - Current Status 6/16/2025', 'Phase 1 to Phase 4', 'In Progress', 'Initial QC Complete', 'SFHA Update', 'Needs Review', 'Not Started'). While fine-grained text like scale details and county names on the map are not transcribed, this is expected for image-based map content in this pipeline. The essential information of the document is preserved.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The primary text element ('Building Footprint Update Status') is identical to the source. The text contained within image descriptions (alt text) accurately reflects the labels and titles seen in the source image (e.g., the date 6/16/2025 and the status categories).

checks.a_analysis: I have carefully reviewed the source document and the provided JSON output. There is no evidence of duplicated text blocks, paragraphs, or images in the converted document. Each major component (title, logo, map) appears exactly once in the output structure.

The source document contains one primary title: 'Building Footprint Update Status'. The JSON output correctly identifies this as the main section title (H1 equivalent). There are no subheadings in the source, and none are incorrectly injected or skipped in the output. The hierarchy is clean and logical.

The source document does not contain any data tables. The map legend, while containing categorical information, is represented graphically. The JSON output correctly identifies zero tables, maintaining structural fidelity.

The reading order in the JSON output follows a logical top-to-bottom flow: it begins with the branding logo (NC Department of Public Safety), proceeds to the main document title, then the primary content (the map image), and concludes with the accreditation footer (EMAP logo). This matches the visual hierarchy of the source document.

There are no textual lists present in the source document. The legend information is contained within the image components and is not extracted as a standalone list, which is an appropriate structural decision for this format.
No links are present in the source document, and the JSON output does not contain any fabricated or injected links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output follows a logical vertical progression that reflects the original document's structure, starting with the main title and followed by the key logos and the central map graphic. While the specific layout of the logos has changed from the bottom corners to the top, and the agency header is missing (see V3), the visual organization remains coherent and information is easily locatable in a similar flow to the source.
The source document does not contain any tables; it consists of a title, branding logos, and a map graphic.

The primary agency branding logo (DPS shield with horse) and associated text ('Emergency Management NC DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY') located in the top-left black header bar of the source PDF are completely missing from the HTML output. This is a meaningful image and text block that identifies the department responsible for the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: DPS shield logo and text 'Emergency Management NC DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY' in the top-left black header bar.
checks.b_evidence_output: The entire top-left branding header is missing from the HTML rendering.

checks.a_analysis: The EMAP Accredited logo and the NC Emergency Management triangle logo are visually duplicated in the HTML output. These logos have been extracted and rendered as standalone images at the top of the document, but they are also visible within the main map image capture that follows, as that graphic encompasses the entire lower portion of the original page where those logos were originally located.
checks.b_evidence_source: The EMAP Accredited logo and NC Emergency Management triangle logo appear once in their respective positions on the page.
checks.b_evidence_output: The EMAP and NC Triangle logos are rendered twice: once as standalone images at the top and again as part of the main map image.
